task_description: Capture the full screen, paste it into an image editor, add the text "DESTOP," and save the edited image as a PNG named "1" on the desktop.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed crop, a semitransparent red X is centered on the rectangular toolbar button showing a small landscape picture icon with a pencil overlay; a tooltip below reads “截图区域”. This is a UI control on the Snipping Tool floating toolbar. In the full-screen view, the Windows desktop is visible with many icons, and the Snipping Tool toolbar is centered near the top of the screen.
caption.action: Click the picture-with-pencil button labeled “截图区域” on the Snipping Tool toolbar.
caption.expectation: The screenshot region mode should become active, switching the tool into area-selection mode so a capture region can be drawn.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed crop shows the Snipping Tool capture-mode dropdown menu, with a semitransparent red X centered on the third option labeled “全屏,” which has a dashed rectangle icon representing full-screen capture. The menu also shows other options above and below such as “矩形,” “窗口,” and “任意多边形.” In the full-screen view, this dropdown is open from the Snipping Tool toolbar near the top center of the Windows desktop, with desktop icons visible in the background.
caption.action: Click the “全屏” full-screen capture option in the Snipping Tool mode dropdown.
caption.expectation: The menu should close and the Snipping Tool should switch to full-screen capture mode, preparing to capture the entire screen.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed crop shows the Windows taskbar search box with a magnifying-glass icon and the placeholder text “搜索,” and the semitransparent red X is centered on this search field. In the full-screen view, the Windows 11 desktop is visible with many application shortcuts on the left, the taskbar along the bottom, and a small screen-recording control widget at the top-right; the target is a taskbar UI control, not a desktop icon.
caption.action: Click the taskbar search box labeled “搜索.”
caption.expectation: The Windows search interface should open, placing focus in the search field so queries can be entered.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view centers on the Windows taskbar search field, which contains the typed text "pain" with the text cursor at the end; this appears to be appending characters into an already focused search box rather than replacing existing text. In the full-screen view, the Windows 11 search panel is open above the taskbar with category tabs across the top, results listed on the left, and a details pane on the right currently showing the Notepad app ("记事本") and recent files; the desktop with many shortcut icons remains visible behind the search panel.
caption.action: Type "PAINT" into the Windows search box.
caption.expectation: The search query should update to include the newly typed letters, and the search results should refresh to show Paint-related matches, likely including the Paint app.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the Windows taskbar search box with a magnifying-glass icon and the typed query "paint"; a semitransparent red X sits over the search field, but the action text indicates a keyboard action. Just above the box is the input-candidate bar showing "1 paint" followed by Chinese candidates. In the full-screen view, the Windows 11 search panel is open with category tabs across the top. In the results list, the best match is the Paint app labeled "画图" with the app icon area on the left, while other results include files and web results containing "The Painter." The desktop remains visible behind the search panel.
caption.action: Press Enter.
caption.expectation: The highlighted default search result should be activated, likely launching the Paint app from the search results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blank white row in the search results list, directly above the visible text result "The Painter - Carl House." In the full-screen view, the Windows 11 Search panel is open with the query "paint" in the taskbar search box. The left results area shows the Paint app ("画图") as the best match at the top, then a folder result, then a web result, then document results including "0102M The Painter - Carl House.qdsav." The click target is a blank region inside the results list, not on a specific icon or labeled control.
caption.action: Click the blank white area in the search results list above the "The Painter - Carl House" entry.
caption.expectation: Focus should remain within the Search window, with no major change beyond possibly clearing any hover state or selecting the list area without opening a result.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the entire Microsoft Paint window rather than a specific control. A large blank white drawing canvas is open in the center with a tiny dark dot near the middle, and the canvas has resize handles around its border. No text entry field or caret is visible, indicating this is a paste operation into the canvas rather than typing into a box. In the full-screen view, Paint is active on a Windows 11 desktop with the ribbon toolbar across the top, including brushes, shapes, and a color palette; desktop icons are visible along the left edge outside the app.
caption.action: Press Ctrl+V to paste the current clipboard contents into the Paint canvas.
caption.expectation: The clipboard content should appear on the canvas, typically as a newly pasted image or selection ready to be positioned or edited.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the Microsoft Paint ribbon's Tools section. A semitransparent red X is centered on the Text tool button, represented by a capital letter A, with a small tooltip above reading "文本(T)". Nearby are other tool icons such as a pencil, paint bucket, eraser, eyedropper, and magnifier. In the full-screen view, Paint is open with a pasted screenshot of the Windows desktop on the canvas; the ribbon is visible across the top, and the target is clearly a toolbar control rather than the canvas.
caption.action: Click the Text tool button with the capital letter A in the toolbar.
caption.expectation: Paint should switch to text-entry mode, indicating the Text tool is selected and ready for placing a text box on the canvas.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows a semitransparent red X placed on a plain light-blue area of the Windows desktop wallpaper with no icon, handle, text box border, or other visible control underneath, indicating a click on a blank canvas region. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with a pasted screenshot of the desktop on the canvas. The Text tool is already selected, and the text formatting bar is visible with font, size, bold, italic, underline, alignment, and background options. The click target is inside the image on the canvas, not on the ribbon or a desktop icon.
caption.action: Click the blank light-blue area on the canvas image to place a text insertion area there.
caption.expectation: Paint should create a text box at that spot and enter text editing mode on the canvas, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a small active text box on the Paint canvas over the pasted desktop screenshot. Inside the text box, lowercase letters are being entered and the visible text reads "desto" with an insertion caret at the end; a Chinese input candidate bar is open directly beneath it, indicating active text composition rather than a finished label. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with the Text tool selected and the text formatting bar visible above the canvas. The text box sits over the blue Windows 11 wallpaper area of the pasted desktop image, confirming this is typing into the newly placed text box and appending characters there.
caption.action: Type "DESTOP" into the active text box on the canvas.
caption.expectation: The text in the Paint text box should update to include the typed word, with the caret remaining in the text box and the IME candidate bar possibly continuing to appear until input is committed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows an active text box on the Paint canvas containing the text "de'stop" with a text caret inside it and resize handles around the box. A Chinese IME candidate bar is directly below the text box, with the first candidate displayed as "的stop," indicating text composition is still active. Although a semitransparent red X is visible over the text area, the raw action is a key press, so this step is keyboard input rather than a click. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with the Text tool formatting bar visible at the top, and the text box sits over a pasted Windows desktop screenshot on the right side of the canvas.
caption.action: Press Enter to confirm the current IME composition in the active text box.
caption.expectation: The selected Chinese input candidate should be committed into the text box, and the composed text should update from the temporary IME state to finalized text.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X on a plain light-blue section of the Windows 11 wallpaper with no visible icon, text, handle, or text-box border underneath, indicating a click on a blank region of the canvas rather than on an object or control point. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with a pasted desktop screenshot on the canvas and the text formatting toolbar still visible near the top, consistent with ongoing text editing work on the image.
caption.action: Click the blank light-blue area on the canvas image.
caption.expectation: Paint should place or confirm a text insertion area at that spot and keep the canvas in text-editing mode, ready for further text entry.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the leftmost top menu label in Paint, the Chinese text "文件" (File), inside the app's menu bar near the upper-left corner. The surrounding menu labels "编辑" and "查看" are visible to its right, confirming the target is a toolbar/menu control rather than the canvas. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with a desktop screenshot on the canvas and the text formatting toolbar still visible, indicating the user is working in Paint and is now clicking the File menu in the top area.
caption.action: Click the "文件" (File) menu in the top menu bar.
caption.expectation: Paint should open the File menu or file-related options panel, shifting focus from canvas editing to document commands.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the file-menu row labeled "另存为" with a floppy-disk-and-pencil style icon to its left. This row is highlighted, and a submenu is already open to the right listing save format options such as "PNG 图片", "JPEG 图片", "BMP 图片", and "GIF 图片", confirming the target is the parent Save As menu item rather than a canvas object. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with the "文件" menu expanded on the left over the image canvas, which contains a pasted desktop screenshot and a small text label on it; the click is occurring in the top-left menu area within Paint.
caption.action: Click the "另存为" (Save As) menu item in the File menu.
caption.expectation: The Save As submenu should remain open or gain focus, allowing selection of a specific image format to save the file.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the first item in the Save As submenu, labeled "PNG 图片". It is a menu entry in the right-side flyout list, above "JPEG 图片", "BMP 图片", and "GIF 图片", so the target is a UI control rather than the canvas. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with the "文件" menu expanded on the left and the "另存为" submenu open beside it over the image canvas, indicating the user is choosing an output format from Paint's file menu.
caption.action: Click the "PNG 图片" option in the Save As submenu.
caption.expectation: Paint should open a Save As dialog for saving the current image as a PNG file.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the entire Save As dialog without any red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click. The dialog is titled "另存为" and is focused on "此电脑" with storage locations shown in the main pane, including "WPS云盘", "Data (D:)", and "Windows-SSD (C:)". At the bottom, the filename field contains a highlighted PNG filename, the save type is "PNG (*.png)", and the lower-right area has "保存(S)" and "取消" buttons. In the full-screen view, this Save As dialog sits on top of Microsoft Paint, which remains open behind it with the ribbon and color palette visible; the desktop is also visible around the app window. No obvious list movement or zoom indicator change is visible, so the scroll appears to be aimed at the active Save As window content.
caption.action: Scroll up within the active Save As dialog.
caption.expectation: The Save As dialog content should move upward if a scrollable area is under the pointer, or otherwise remain unchanged while focus stays in the save window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the left navigation item labeled "桌面" with a small monitor-like icon, indicating the Desktop location in the Save As dialog sidebar. The item is highlighted, so this is a UI control in the dialog's navigation pane. In the full-screen view, the "另存为" Save As window is open over Microsoft Paint, with the file name field containing "无标题.png" and the save type set to "PNG (*.png)"; the main pane currently shows storage locations under "此电脑".
caption.action: Click the "桌面" item in the Save As dialog's left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The Save As dialog should navigate to the Desktop folder, updating the main file list to show desktop contents while keeping the current filename and PNG format.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the filename text field in the Save As dialog with the current name "无标题.png" highlighted in blue, indicating the drag begins on selected filename text, likely to adjust the text selection. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open with a Save As window titled "另存为" focused on the Desktop folder; the file list is visible in the main pane, the filename field and PNG file type selector are at the bottom, and the Save and Cancel buttons are on the lower right.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the highlighted filename text "无标题.png" in the file name field, drag along the shown path across the text selection area to adjust the selected range, then release.
caption.expectation: The text selection inside the filename field should change to match the dragged range, preparing part or all of the filename for editing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the file name text box containing the text "无标题.png" in the Save As dialog. This is a text-entry control at the bottom of the dialog, not a toolbar button or canvas object. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open behind a "另存为" Save As window set to the Desktop folder, with several folders listed in the main pane and the file name field active above the file type dropdown "PNG (*.png)".
caption.action: Click the file name text box containing "无标题.png".
caption.expectation: The file name field should become active for editing, placing the text cursor inside or confirming focus in the text box so the filename can be edited.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the Save As dialog’s file name input area near the bottom. The text field labeled "文件名(N):" is active, and the visible content in the box is now ".png", indicating the previous filename has been replaced and a single character is being entered at the start of the new name. The save type field below still shows "PNG (*.png)". In the full-screen view, this dialog is open on the Desktop inside Microsoft Paint, with desktop folders listed in the main pane and the "保存(S)" and "取消" buttons at the lower right.
caption.action: Type "1" into the active file name field, replacing the previous selected filename content so the new name begins as "1.png".
caption.expectation: The file name field should update to show the typed character as the new filename base, resulting in a filename like "1.png" while keeping the PNG file type unchanged.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the "保存(S)" button, a rectangular Save control in the lower-right area of the Save As dialog, with a neighboring "取消" button to its right. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Paint is open behind the "另存为" dialog focused on the Desktop folder; the file name field contains "1.png" and the save type remains "PNG (*.png)".
caption.action: Click the "保存(S)" button.
caption.expectation: The Save As dialog should close and the image should be saved to the Desktop with the filename "1.png", returning focus to the Paint window.
